bead.title: work: preflight project-local skills before long-running entrypoints
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Long-running DDx entrypoints can start work even when the project-local skill install is broken. In /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau, post-attempt triage logged `triage hook: skill missing: bead-lifecycle (continuing)` only after an execution failed. The operator had to infer that the project still had a pre-migration skill layout and needed `ddx update --force` / `ddx doctor`.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/cmd/work.go:106` delegates directly to `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl` without a lightweight runtime preflight. `cli/cmd/try.go:102-120` resolves execution flags and proceeds toward store/runtime setup without checking project-local skill prerequisites. `cli/cmd/server.go:98-146` starts the server without surfacing degraded project-local skill layout. `cli/cmd/doctor.go:281-287` already detects legacy skill symlinks and prints `run: ddx update --force`, but that diagnostic is only available when the operator explicitly runs `ddx doctor`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a shared lightweight project-runtime preflight helper for long-running entrypoints. It should reuse existing doctor/install helper logic where practical, but must not run the full `ddx doctor` checks.
- Wire the helper into `ddx work`, `ddx try`, compatibility `ddx agent execute-loop`, and `ddx server` before execution begins.
- For work/try/execute-loop: fail on missing required project/tracker state, warn once per process for optional lifecycle-hook degradation in WARN-ONLY mode, and include remediation.
- For server: fail on required server project state, but log degraded startup diagnostics for missing lifecycle skills or legacy skill symlink layout.
- Checked paths for `bead-lifecycle` must be `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` and `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` under the resolved project root.
- Remediation text must include `ddx update --force` first, then `ddx doctor`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add network, package-manager, route/provider, stale-doc, or Fizeau health probes to this preflight.
- Do not change bead-lifecycle hook classification semantics.
- Do not make `ddx server` refuse to start solely because `bead-lifecycle` is missing.
- Do not edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` manually; tracker mutation is handled by this bead record only.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-a9d130d0. No deps.
bead.acceptance:
1. `TestProjectRuntimePreflight_MissingBeadLifecycleReportsCheckedPathsAndRemediation` verifies a project with `.agents/skills/ddx/SKILL.md` but no nested `bead-lifecycle` reports the resolved project root, both checked nested skill paths, `ddx update --force`, and `ddx doctor`.
2. `TestWorkPreflight_WarnsOnceForMissingLifecycleSkill` verifies `ddx work --once` emits one preflight warning before dispatch and does not repeat the warning across loop iterations in the same process.
3. `TestTryPreflight_WarnsBeforeClaimForMissingLifecycleSkill` verifies `ddx try <id>` runs the preflight before claim/worktree setup and includes the remediation text.
4. `TestServerPreflight_MissingLifecycleSkillStartsDegraded` verifies `ddx server` surfaces degraded startup diagnostics without blocking server construction/startup solely because `bead-lifecycle` is missing.
5. `TestProjectRuntimePreflight_LegacySkillSymlinkSuggestsUpdateForce` verifies legacy project-local skill symlinks reuse the FEAT-015 remediation path: `ddx update --force`, then `ddx doctor`.
6. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... -run 'Test(ProjectRuntimePreflight|WorkPreflight|TryPreflight|ServerPreflight)'` green.
7. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/...` green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

events.body: The shared project-runtime preflight and its cmd tests are implemented and the targeted preflight tests pass, but the required broader gate `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/...` still fails in pre-existing `cmd` execute-bead coverage with `excluding execute-bead cleanup metadata: exit status 128` / related `TestExecuteBead*` failures that are outside the preflight change scope. done: - Added a shared lightweight project-runtime preflight helper in `cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight.go`. - Wired the helper into `ddx work`, `ddx try`, `ddx agent execute-loop`, and `ddx server`. - Added focused tests for missing bead-lifecycle, legacy symlink remediation, work, try, and server behavior in `cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight_test.go`. verified: - `go test ./cmd -run 'Test(ProjectRuntimePreflight|WorkPreflight|TryPreflight|ServerPreflight)'` - `go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server -run 'Test(ProjectRuntimePreflight|WorkPreflight|TryPreflight|ServerPreflight)'` blocked: - `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/...` still fails on existing execute-bead cases unrelated to the preflight helper. follow_up: - Investigate the existing `TestExecuteBeadContextBudgetFromConfig`, `TestInvokeExecuteBeadFromLoopParsesJSONAmidWarnings`, and `TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidence*` failures in the broader `cmd` suite before attempting a commit.

{"base_rev":"f54eda56b6ac709e39c18eddaaf2d16178a50818","classification":"tests_red","detail":"no_changes","rationale":"The implementation and focused preflight tests passed, but the required broader gate `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/...` still fails in pre-existing `cmd` execute-bead coverage (`TestExecuteBead*` / cleanup metadata exit status 128). The bead is therefore blocked by failing tests outside the preflight scope, not by missing implementation changes.","recommended_action":"investigate","result_rev":"f54eda56b6ac709e39c18eddaaf2d16178a50818","session_id":"eb-d9308084","status":"no_changes","suggested_amendments":"No code amendment needed for the preflight change itself; isolate and fix the existing `cmd` execute-bead test failures, then rerun the full gate.","suggested_followup_beads":[{"title":"investigate cmd: execute-bead suite failures blocking full preflight gate"}]}

no_changes
rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: The shared project-runtime preflight and its cmd tests are implemented and the targeted preflight tests pass, but the required broader gate `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/...` still fails in pre-existing `cmd` execute-bead coverage with `excluding execute-bead cleanup metadata: exit status 128` / related `TestExecuteBead*` failures that are outside the preflight change scope.

done:
- Added a shared lightweight project-runtime preflight helper in `cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight.go`.
- Wired the helper into `ddx work`, `ddx try`, `ddx agent execute-loop`, and `ddx server`.
- Added focused tests for missing bead-lifecycle, legacy symlink remediation, work, try, and server behavior in `cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight_test.go`.

verified:
- `go test ./cmd -run 'Test(ProjectRuntimePreflight|WorkPreflight|TryPreflight|ServerPreflight)'`
- `go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server -run 'Test(ProjectRuntimePreflight|WorkPreflight|TryPreflight|ServerPreflight)'`

blocked:
- `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/...` still fails on existing execute-bead cases unrelated to the preflight helper.

follow_up:
- Investigate the existing `TestExecuteBeadContextBudgetFromConfig`, `TestInvokeExecuteBeadFromLoopParsesJSONAmidWarnings`, and `TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidence*` failures in the broader `cmd` suite before attempting a commit.

{"rationale":"Bead passes 7 of 8 criteria. Title is imperative and names subsystem+change. Description has PROBLEM, ROOT CAUSE with file:line references (cli/cmd/work.go:106, cli/cmd/try.go:102-120, cli/cmd/server.go:98-146, cli/cmd/doctor.go:281-287), PROPOSED FIX, and NON-SCOPE. AC is numbered 1-8 with five named Test* functions, a -run regex filter, a broad go test gate, and lefthook. Labels carry phase:N, area:*, kind:*, and spec cross-refs. Self-test passes — a cold agent can identify files, line numbers, and test targets without operator hand-curation. FAIL: criterion 7 (Parent + Deps) has a direct contradiction. The description body ends with 'PARENT + DEPS — Parent: ddx-a9d130d0. No deps.' but the bead metadata lists deps: [\"ddx-11f0cbf2\"]. An autonomous agent reading only the description body would believe there are no blocking deps and may start work before ddx-11f0cbf2 is closed, violating the DAG contract.","score":7,"suggested_fixes":["Reconcile the description's PARENT + DEPS section with the metadata: replace 'No deps.' with 'Deps: ddx-11f0cbf2 (must be closed first).' so the description is the single source of truth for an agent reading it cold.","Alternatively, if ddx-11f0cbf2 is no longer a real blocker, remove it from the deps array in the metadata and leave the description unchanged."],"waivers_applied":[]}
